title: Unable to specify extrahosts as part of the build API
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->
The Podman API does not like the `extrahosts` parameter when executing an image build. This scenario is typically exercised by build extensions (Gradle/Maven/etc.) where the the tooling interacts with the engine via the APIs. The same scenario was working as expected with the Docker Engine APIs. Given that the APIs were meant to be compatible, I believe this is a bug. Below, I have created a simple test case that verifies this scenario.

_p.s.: I wanted to take a stab at providing a fix, then realized that there are no instructions for building the code on a Mac._

### Podman Version
```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.8
Built:        Wed Nov  9 12:43:58 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Built:        Fri Nov 11 07:01:27 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Test Case
```
cat > Dockerfile << EOF
FROM ubuntu:focal
RUN mkdir demo
RUN apt-get update
RUN echo "Hello There!"
EOF

tar -cvf Dockerfile.tar.gz Dockerfile 
```

**Successful Scenario**
```
curl -v -H "Content-Type:application/tar" --data-binary '@Dockerfile.tar.gz' --unix-socket $DOCKER_HOST "http:2375/build?t=build_test"
```

**Failure Scenario**
```
curl -v -H "Content-Type:application/tar" --data-binary '@Dockerfile.tar.gz' --unix-socket $DOCKER_HOST "http:2375/build?t=build_test&extrahosts=host.custom.internal%3A10.10.10.10"
```

comments.body:
OSX build instructions: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/build_osx.md
But this will likely not help much because you need to fix the server side which only runs on linux.

Looking at the code the option accepts a single input that as format as json array.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go#L240-L246

I agree that should accept the docker format but it should also keep the backwards compat with the json format to not break existing users.

@Luap99 - Thank you for looking into it. I have some more information.

* I did notice the array structure.
* When multiple `extrahosts` are provided to the tooling, it adds them as additional query params, i.e.,
```
&extrahosts=host1%3A10.10.10.10&extrahosts=host2%3A10.10.10.10
```
* Looks like the implementation does not serialize multiple entries of the same query param as an array
  * this scenario also failed
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Podman team, do you have any suggestions on how to proceed here?

>     * When multiple `extrahosts` are provided to the tooling, it adds them as additional query params, i.e.,
> 
> 
> ```
> &extrahosts=host1%3A10.10.10.10&extrahosts=host2%3A10.10.10.10
> ```
> 
>     * Looks like the implementation does not serialize multiple entries of the same query param as an array

The HTTP protocol and related standards do not specify a serialization method for arrays.
Historically, various programming languages have implemented different serialization technics :

* PHP suffixes such parameters with `[]` (consistent with the syntax used in PHP code to append an element to an array).
* Meanwhile, most Python-based frameworks simply repeat the parameter's name for every array element (same as in your example).

I think the representation currently used by podman is fine: it just happens to follow the Python semantics in that regard.
But maybe someone from the actual development team can shed some light on this particular design decision.

I finally got some time to look at my open items. Would the community be open to accepting a fix for this issue? My plan was to ...

Modify the deserialization behavior to do the following,
- maintain current contract / behavior of deserializing the content as an array
- if that fails, then attempt to deserialize it as a string
- repeat the above logic for all occurrences of the query parameter

Let me know if you would open to such a fix within the code.
